headlines.title: Libya Declares Major Oil Discovery Commercial as Production Revival Gains Pace
headlines.summary: Libya’s National Oil Corporation, NOC, and Austrian energy firm OMV have declared the Essar oil discovery commercially viable as OPEC’s second-largest African producer pushes to revive its industry in partnerships with international oil majors. headlines.title: Pakistan Plans Fresh LNG Purchases Amid Strait of Hormuz Shutdown
headlines.summary: Pakistan is looking to buy additional spot LNG cargoes for July and August delivery as the fresh halt to Strait of Hormuz traffic has disrupted shipments from its term supplier, Qatar. headlines.title: Oil Prices Set for Biggest Weekly Surge Since April as Iran War Escalates
headlines.summary: Oil prices were headed early on Friday for a 12% weekly gain, the biggest jump in one week since April, as the re-escalation in the Middle East and the abrupt halt to the recovery of Strait of Hormuz flows pushed crude futures prices to the highest in over a month. headlines.title: China Could Be About to Remove Oil's Biggest Safety Net
headlines.summary: The oil market may soon run out of the supply and demand cushions that have kept prices from soaring to record highs during the huge loss of flows through the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: India, South Africa To Boost Their Strategic Petroleum Reserves
headlines.summary: Back in March, the 32-member countries of the International Energy Agency (IEA) agreed to a record release of 400 million barrels of oil and refined products from their emergency reserves after the Iran war disrupted global energy flows and triggered sharp oil price spikes. headlines.title: IEA Predicts First Decrease in Global Oil Demand Since 2020
headlines.summary: Global oil demand could decrease for the first time since 2020, following months of oil trade restrictions in the Middle East and ongoing geopolitical turmoil, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on 10th July. headlines.title: Chevron Moves Closer to Iraq's Biggest Oil Prize—and a Hormuz Exit Strategy
headlines.summary: Chevron is taking another step toward expanding its footprint in Iraq, and is set to sign two memoranda of understanding on Friday that will move the U.S. supermajor closer to developing the giant West Qurna 2 oilfield and the Nassiriya project. headlines.title: Yemen's Houthis Threaten New Oil Shock as Civil War Reignites
headlines.summary: Yemen's Houthi Rebels are threatening to make the global energy crisis worse at the same time that the nation's own energy security is in dire straits. headlines.title: Eni CEO: Big Oil Bets on Southeast Asia and Latin America
headlines.summary: The oil and gas industry is shifting its capital investments toward Southeast Asia and Latin America due to prolonged shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, Eni S.p.A (NYSE:E) CEO Claudio Descalzi has revealed. Speaking to a parliamentary committee on Thursday, the leader

headlines.title: EU Sets Target To Double Electrification Rate, Cut Oil & Gas Use
headlines.summary: The European Union has unveiled the Electrification Action Plan, aiming for electricity to account for 46% of total energy consumption within the bloc by 2040, effectively doubling the current rate.
